## About Engagement Dashboards

The Engagement Dashboards are a powerful component of [Thinkific Analytics](https://support.thinkific.com/hc/en-us/articles/360040037093) to help you grow your business smarter. Get the data and insights you need to measure engagement, revenue, and overall program performance while empowering your team to make impactful decisions.

- **Actionable Insights for Enhanced Learning Products**

Improve your learning products with actionable course, student, quiz and survey engagement insights - create products your customers actually care about and are willing to stay/pay for -accelerating your path to growth.

- **Data-Driven Proof of Impact and ROI**

Prove your work is making a difference - use business performance data to understand trends in your business that help you plan, optimize, and really move the needle to drive ROI.

- **Empowering Teams with Data and Insights**

Unlock data and insights for your entire team - give your Course Admins, Group Analysts, and B2B Partners access to Thinkific Analytics and empower more people to make impactful data-driven decisions quickly.

## Accessing Your Engagement Dashboards

1. From your Admin Dashboard, select **Analytics**
2. Select **Engagement** in the dropdown menu
3. Select the desired tab

## Giving Users Access to Your Engagement Dashboards

Access this feature on Thinkific Plus!

##### Curious, but not yet on Plus?

##### Discover how Plus can supercharge your business growth.

[Book a call](https://www.thinkific.com/plus/plus-customer-enquiries/?utm_source=knowledge_base&utm_medium=help_center&utm_campaign=analytics_access)

As a [Site Owner](https://support.thinkific.com/hc/en-us/articles/360030353794), you have the ability to assign roles to users, granting them access to the Course, Quiz, Student Performance, Survey, and SCORM [Engagement Dashboards](https://support.thinkific.com/hc/en-us/articles/15795863469463), as well as the [Enrollments Dashboard](https://support.thinkific.com/hc/en-us/articles/15795863469463-Thinkific-Analytics-Engagement-Dashboards#enrollments) within Thinkific Analytics. By designating individuals as [Site Admins](https://support.thinkific.com/hc/en-us/articles/360030735453), [Course Admins](https://support.thinkific.com/hc/en-us/articles/360030735613), or [Group Analysts](https://support.thinkific.com/hc/en-us/articles/360030735853), you can provide them with tailored insights and empower them to make data-driven decisions.

Site Admins get comprehensive access to all Thinkific Analytics dashboards, enabling them to gain a holistic view of engagement across the entire platform. Group Analysts and Course Admins have restricted access to Engagement and [Enrollment Dashboards](https://support.thinkific.com/hc/en-us/articles/360040037093#enrollments), limiting their visibility to data specific to the Course or Group they are associated with.

This feature allows designated users to analyze engagement metrics, identify trends, and optimize their respective courses or groups to foster better student outcomes. By granting selective access to the Engagement Dashboards, you can distribute responsibilities, collaborate effectively, and enhance the overall learning experience for your students.

Learn more about [How to Assign a User Role](https://support.thinkific.com/hc/en-us/articles/360045347033)

## Understanding Your Engagement Dashboards

**Overview Dashboard**

The Engagement Overview Dashboard provides a comprehensive view of your students' engagement. It enables you to monitor engagement trends over time, identify the most successful learning products, keep tabs on the number of actively enrolled students, and even rank students based on their engagement levels. This dashboard is designed to assist you and your team in making informed, data-driven decisions to enhance the learning experience and improve the overall effectiveness of your product offerings.

![](https://support.thinkific.com/hc/article_attachments/18261621260439)

- **Engagement Trends**

This chart shows the unique count of students who engaged with a course or community during the chosen time frame and for the chosen filters. You can change the period to daily, weekly, monthly, quarterly, or yearly using the Date Granularity filter. Engagement includes viewing or completing a lesson or interaction with communities including posting, commenting, replying to comments, and liking a post, comment, or reply.

- **Engagement by Product**

This chart shows the courses or communities with the highest number of unique students engaged during the chosen timeframe and for the chosen filters.

- **Active Enrollments and Active Users**

This chart shows two metrics at each point in time: Active Enrollments (the number of enrollments that had access to a course or community) and Active Users (the number of unique students who had access). A student is considered to have access at a given point in time if they were enrolled and their enrollment expiry date was on or after that date. The chart reflects the selected timeframe and filters, and the Date Granularity filter lets you adjust the view to daily, weekly, monthly, quarterly, or yearly intervals.

- **Engagement by Student**

This table ranks students by the number of days each student has engaged with courses or communities in the chosen timeframe and for the chosen filters.

**Course Dashboard**

The Course Dashboard uncovers comprehensive insights into your students' interactions with your courses. This technical dashboard offers a wealth of data to help you make informed decisions and adjustments to adapt and optimize your course content for improved student engagement. These metrics allow you to analyze enrollment patterns, pinpoint disengaged students, and tailor your program to better cater to your audience. With the Course Dashboard, you can fine-tune your teaching strategies, ensuring that your students remain actively engaged and motivated in their educational journey.

> If a student has recently enrolled or progressed through a course chapter or lesson, you may need to wait up to 24 hours to see this activity in your engagement reports. Reporting data is updated daily at 4 a.m. PDT.

![Analytics - Engagement Course Dec 25.jpg](https://support.thinkific.com/hc/article_attachments/25618098342039)

- **Total Enrollments**

Count of enrollments that were created in the chosen date ranges and based on the chosen filters. This includes all active and expired course enrollments.

- **Active Enrollments**

Count of enrollments that were created in the chosen date ranges that are still not expired.

- **Expired Enrollments**

Count of enrollments that were created in the chosen date ranges that are expired.

- **Enrollments Not Engaged**

The number of enrollments that have not viewed or completed any course content, indicating potential disengagement or lack of progress within your students.

- **% Course Completed**

Categories students based on their Lesson Completion Rate to help you understand the progress and engagement levels of your students. The Lesson Completion Rate is the percentage of the lessons of a course a student has completed.

- **Monthly Cohort Progress**

This table displays the average progress of enrollments, grouped by the month of their enrollment date. The Average Progress may represent either the completion rate or the view rate of the course, depending on the metric selected in the Progress Metric Selector filter.

- **Bundle Progress**

This table displays the average progress of enrollments, grouped by the bundles they are enrolled in. The Average Progress may represent either the completion rate or the view rate of the course, depending on the metric selected in the Progress Metric Selector filter.

- **Group Progress**

This table displays the average progress of enrollments, grouped by the group the enrolled student belongs to, if any. The Average Progress may represent either the completion rate or the view rate of the course, depending on the metric selected in the Progress Metric Selector filter.

- **Lesson Engagement**

Summarizes engagement information for each lesson by providing details on lesson engagement, including the number of students who viewed and completed each lesson, [average time spent on lessons](https://support.thinkific.com/hc/en-us/articles/35496252391319-Understanding-the-Student-Time-Spent-Metric), and more. This data helps you identify trends in student engagement and optimize lesson design accordingly.

**Community Dashboard**

The Communities Dashboard empowers Creators utilizing [Communities](https://support.thinkific.com/hc/en-us/articles/7265849758999) to gain valuable insights into their member base and community engagement. With this dashboard, you can access key metrics and data to effectively manage and optimize your community. A comprehensive view of your community's activity allows you to gauge the level of engagement and identify the most active and top contributing members, and the impacts of their interactions. Whether you're seeking to nurture participation, track the success of your community and spaces, or simply understand the dynamics within it, the Communities Dashboard equips you with the insights required for effective community management and growth.

![](https://support.thinkific.com/hc/article_attachments/18261621266711)

- **Contributing Members**

Count of unique members who have contributed to the community in the chosen timeframe and for the chosen filters. Contributions include posting, commenting under a post, replying to a comment, and liking posts, comments, or replies.

- **Contributions**

Count of contributions by members in the chosen timeframe and for the chosen filters.

- **Cross-Member Contributions**

Count of contributions by members to posts in the chosen timeframe and for the chosen filters. Cross-member contributions include commenting under a post, replying to a comment, and liking posts, comments, or replies.

- **Contributing Members Trend**

Count of unique contributing members over time in the chosen timeframe and for the chosen filters.

- **Contributions Trend**

Count of contributions over time broken down by the type of contribution (posts, comments, replies, likes) in the chosen timeframe and for the chosen Filters.

- **Contribution Heatmap**

The contribution heatmap shows the count of contributions for each day of the week for each week in the chosen timeframe and chosen filters. The heatmap has a 52-week limit. If a wider date range is chosen only the most recent 52 weeks are shown.

- **Communities**

Count of interactions in each community in the chosen timeframe and for the chosen filters.

- **Spaces**

Count of posts created in each space in the chosen timeframe and for the chosen filters.

- **Members**

Count of contributions by each member in the chosen timeframe and for the chosen filters sorted by the highest contributing member.

**Video Dashboard**

The Video Dashboard feature provides you with valuable insights into the interaction of your students with videos within your courses. By utilizing video analytics, you can easily identify the videos with the highest play rates and pinpoint the moments when students tend to drop off. This technical tool enables you to determine which videos are captivating and engaging for your audience, while also identifying areas that may require refinement. With this data-driven approach, you can make informed improvements to your content, enhancing the overall learning experience for your students.

Learn more about the [Video Dashboard](https://support.thinkific.com/hc/en-us/articles/360059523693).

![](https://support.thinkific.com/hc/article_attachments/15867086391959)

**Quiz Dashboard**

Effortlessly access and analyze comprehensive reports on quiz performance to better understand your student’s engagement and performance with quizzes. Gain valuable insights into overall statistics and delve into the specifics of each question and answer while gaining insights into how users are progressing through their learning journey. Make data-driven decisions with comprehensive information to empower you to evaluate quiz effectiveness, optimize quizzes, and enhance the learning experience for your students.

![Analytics - Engagement Quiz - Dec 25.jpg](https://support.thinkific.com/hc/article_attachments/37133042364183)

- **Average Score**

The average score of all students who have completed a quiz based on the selected filters. This only includes quizzes that students have completed.

- **Unique Student Completed**

The unique count of students who have completed a quiz based on the selected filters.

- **Score Distribution**

Student scores are distributed in 10% increments based on the selected filters. This only includes quizzes that students have completed.

- **Most Completed Quiz**

Top 30 most completed quizzes based on the selected filters.

- **Number of Attempts**

Student's quiz attempts distribution based on the selected filter. Each time a student starts a quiz and completes it will be counted as an attempt. This only includes quizzes that students have completed.

- **Chapters by Quiz Average Score**

Average student score in each chapter based on the selected filters. This only includes quizzes that students have completed.

- **Quiz Performance**

Quiz performance shows students' performance on each quiz based on the selected filters. This includes completion count, average score, pass rate, and number of questions currently in the quiz. This only includes quizzes that students have completed.

- **Question Performance**

Question performance shows students' performance on each question based on the selected filters. This includes attempt count, successful attempts, count of correct answers, count of incorrect answers, and correct answer ratio. This only includes quizzes that students have completed.

**Survey Dashboard**

View and analyze comprehensive survey results to better understand your students’ experiences, motivations, and feedback. The Survey Dashboard brings all response data together in one clear, centralized view - allowing you to quickly identify completion trends and explore individual questions. Understand how completion rate changes over time and by a survey, and see all individual responses at a glance without leaving Thinkific.

![Analytics - Engagement Survey - Dec 25.jpg](https://support.thinkific.com/hc/article_attachments/37133026176407)

- **Students enrolled**

Count of students who are enrolled in a course that contains an accessible survey within the specified time frame, course, and group.

- **Students completed a survey**

Count of students who have recorded at least one completed survey attempt within the specified time frame, course, and group.

- **Students with no surveys completed**

Count of students who are actively enrolled in a course (as defined by "Students enrolled"), but who have not recorded any completed survey attempts within the specified time frame, course, and group.

- **Survey started**

Count of all survey attempts initiated by students within the specified time frame, course, and group. Note: A single student may contribute more than once to this count, as they can start multiple surveys.

- **Surveys completed**

Count of all survey attempts marked as complete by students within the specified time frame, course, and group. Note: A single student may contribute more than once to this count as they can complete multiple surveys.

- **Completion rate**

The rate of surveys completed over the total surveys started within the specified time frame, course, and group. (Calculated as: Surveys Completed / Survey Started).

- **Completion rate over time**

A chart illustrating the trend of survey engagement by plotting the count of surveys started, surveys completed, and the resulting completion rate (calculated as: Surveys Completed / Survey Started). The view can be adjusted (daily, weekly, etc.) using the date granularity filter for the specified time frame, course, and group.

- **Completion rate by survey**

A table summarizing the performance of each individual survey. For each survey, it displays the count of attempts, the count of enrolled students who attempted it, the count of completions, and the overall rate of completion (calculated as: Surveys Completed / Survey Started). The table is ordered by the total number of attempts within the specified time frame, course, and group.

- **Response distribution**

A table summarizing the frequency and distribution of all answer choices for every question across all surveys. The data is organized and sorted by the survey name, the question's position within the survey, and the position of the corresponding choice or scale option within the specified time frame, course, and group.

- **Response details**

A table providing a summary of the specific, completed answers submitted by students for every question across all surveys within the specified time frame, course, and group.


**Student Performance Dashboard**

The Student Performance Dashboard gives visibility into each learner's progress over time, allowing you to track their advancement and identify specific areas where additional support or resources may be required. This level of insight empowers you to personalize your content and learning experiences according to the unique needs of your students. By tailoring your approach, you can enhance engagement and boost retention rates, ensuring that they receive the optimal support they need to succeed.

![](https://support.thinkific.com/hc/article_attachments/15867086389655)

- **Student Information**

This table contains the student’s name, email, count of course and community enrollments, count of completed course enrollments, student sign-up, and last sign-in date.

- **Course Engagement**

This table provides a summarized view of a student’s interaction with the course. It includes information on course progress, enrollment date, start date, and course completion date in case the student has completed the course.

- **Lesson Engagement**

This table provides a summarized view of the student’s interaction with the lesson. It includes information on the approximate time spent on the lesson, whether the student has completed the lesson, and the completion date.

**SCORM Dashboard**

Available on Thinkific Plus plans

##### Connect with us to learn more

[Book a call](https://www.thinkific.com/plus/plus-customer-enquiries/?utm_source=knowledge_base&utm_medium=help_center&utm_campaign=scorm)

> Note that this feature is currently in Beta. If you have any feedback, [please let us know](https://support.thinkific.com/hc/en-us/articles/360030719413)!

The SCORM Dashboard provides reporting for SCORM-compliant courses created using the instructions here: [Create SCORM-Compliant Courses](https://support.thinkific.com/hc/en-us/articles/24607412212119). This dashboard offers a look at the pass/fail rates of your learners in SCORM courses and their scores (where applicable and when provided by the SCORM package configuration). These metrics allow you to analyze learner success rate and tailor your program to better cater to your audience. With the SCORM Dashboard, you can fine-tune your teaching strategies, ensuring that your students remain actively engaged and motivated in their educational journey. Learn more about SCORM reporting here: [SCORM Course Reporting](https://support.thinkific.com/hc/en-us/articles/24607412212119#reporting).

> If a student has recently enrolled or progressed through a course chapter or lesson, you may need to wait up to 24 hours to see this activity in your engagement reports. Reporting data is updated daily at 4 a.m. PDT.

![](https://support.thinkific.com/hc/article_attachments/25599269086871)

- **SCORM Course Enrollments**

Count of enrollments in the specified time frame for the chosen SCORM courses.

- **SCORM Course Enrolled Learners**

Count of learners enrolled in the specified time frame for the chosen SCORM courses.

- **Completion Rate**

A visual of the different completion statuses for learners across a course. Completion statuses are either _Not Attempted_, _Incomplete_, _Complete_, or _Unknown_.

- **Success Rate**

A visual of the different success statuses for learners for a course. Success statuses can be _Pass_, _Fail_, or _Unknown_.

- **SCORM Course Details**

A table summarizing the details of each SCORM course. For each course, it displays the count of enrolled learners, the count of learners who completed the course, the completion rate (calculated as _Completed Learners_/ _Enrolled Learners_), the count of learners who passed the course, the pass rate (calculated as _Passed Learners_/ _Completed Learners_), the passing grade as well as the average time spent for this course by all learners. The table is ordered by Course Name.

- **Average score**

The average score of all learners, ranging between 0 and 100, in the specified time frame for the chosen SCORM courses. Individual score is calculated by dividing the learner's raw score by the maximum score specified in the course. These only include learners enrolments who have completed a course with a SCORM package.

- **Score Distribution**

This chart illustrates the distribution of learners’ scores, categorized in 10 percent increments. These only include learners enrolments who have completed a course with a SCORM package.

- **Average Score By Learner**

A table summarizing the average score of each learner and the number of SCORM courses they are enrolled in for the specified timeframe. The table is ordered by the count of Enrolled Courses.

- **Interactions: Summary Per Description**

A table summarizing how learners are interacting with every description (questions or prompts) in the course, including the average attempts per learner and accuracy rate (correct interactions divided by all interactions). The data is organized and sorted by the course name.

- **Interactions: Summary Per Description and Learner**

A table summarizing each learner’s interaction with every description (questions or prompts) point in the course, including the count of attempts and accuracy rate (correct interactions divided by all interactions). The data is organized and sorted by the ‘Course Name’ and ‘Student Email’.


**Thinker Dashboard**

> This feature is currently in beta. Access is limited during this stage and not widely available.

The Thinker Dashboard provides reporting for [Thinker - AI Teaching Assistant](https://support.thinkific.com/hc/en-us/articles/34681678535191). This dashboard provides insights into its usage and effectiveness with your learners, such as total users, number of responses, estimated hours saved, and most active students.

The analytics page also includes a detailed message history, allowing you to review all conversations or filter them by a specific user to see their interactions, as well as view which responses your learners marked as thumbs up or thumbs down. Learn more about Thinker here: [Thinker - AI Teaching Assistant](https://support.thinkific.com/hc/en-us/articles/34681678535191)

> Some reporting data takes time to populate. Please allow 24 hours for your data to refresh.

![Analytics - Engagement Thinker - Dec 25.jpg](https://support.thinkific.com/hc/article_attachments/37234779924759)

- **Thinker responses**

Total number of messages sent by Thinker, excluding introductions, in the selected time period.

- **Estimated hours saved by AI responses**

Estimated total hours saved by Thinker in the selected time period. The calculation assumes a human would take an average of 5 minutes to answer each message.

- **Users interacting with Thinker**

Total number of unique students that have sent a message to Thinker in the selected time period.

- **Usage over time**

Number of users, volume of responses from Thinker, and average number of responses per user over time. You can adjust the time period with the date granularity filter.

- **User message count**

A list of users who have messaged Thinker and the total number of messages sent during the selected time period.

- **Percentage of total responses that have a reaction**

Percentage of messages that had either a positive (thumbs-up) or negative (thumbs-down) reaction from the user in the selected time period. Reactions are an optional thumbs-up or thumbs-down available on every message sent by Thinker.

- **Reaction Breakdown**

User reactions to messages from Thinker during the selected time period. Users can give each message an optional positive (thumbs-up) or negative (thumbs-down) reaction.

- **Reactions over time**

Positive (thumbs-up) or negative (thumbs-down) user reactions to messages from Thinker during the selected time period. You can adjust the time period with the date granularity filter.

- **Message history**

A complete conversation history between users and Thinker, in the order the messages were sent.

_Use Student Progress Reports to get insights from student activity in your courses and measure completion rates!_

* * *

## In this article:

[About This Feature](https://support.thinkific.com/hc/en-us/articles/360030369974-Progress-Reports#about)

[How It Works](https://support.thinkific.com/hc/en-us/articles/360030369974-Progress-Reports#howitworks)

[Progress Reports](https://support.thinkific.com/hc/en-us/articles/360030369974-Progress-Reports#progress)

[Cohort Reports](https://support.thinkific.com/hc/en-us/articles/360030369974-Progress-Reports#cohort)

[Group Reports](https://support.thinkific.com/hc/en-us/articles/360030369974-Progress-Reports#group)

[Important Considerations](https://support.thinkific.com/hc/en-us/articles/360030369974-Progress-Reports#important)

[Frequently Asked Questions](https://support.thinkific.com/hc/en-us/articles/360030369974-Progress-Reports#FAQ)

## About This Feature

**Progress Reports** provide an overview of student activity in your course. You can see when each student started the course, completed the course, and last signed in, as well as their [view and completion rate](https://support.thinkific.com/hc/en-us/articles/360033331514) of the course.

**Cohort Reports** allow you to compare student completion rates by groups of students with a common start date. For example, all students enrolled in January would be part of the January cohort and you can compare the completion percentage for your January cohort and February cohort. If you have made any changes to your course between cohorts, this helps to measure if your changes affected completion rates!

**Group Reports** are similar to Progress Reports, but can be run for a specific [Group](https://support.thinkific.com/hc/en-us/articles/360030350854). You will be able to see details for all students in a Group including name, email, and completion rates for every course they are enrolled in.

## How It Works

Reports can be viewed in Thinkific or exported as a CSV file. CSV (comma separated values) is a universal file type that can be opened by any spreadsheet program.

When exporting as a CSV file, a link to download your file will be sent to the email address you are currently logged in with. For example, if you are logged in as the [Site Owner](https://support.thinkific.com/hc/en-us/articles/360030353794), it will be sent to your Site Owner email address and if you are logged in as a [Site Admin](https://support.thinkific.com/hc/en-us/articles/360030735453), it will be sent to the Site Admin email address.

The following roles can view **Progress Reports** and **Cohort Reports**:

- [Site Owner](https://support.thinkific.com/hc/en-us/articles/360030353794)
- [Site Admin](https://support.thinkific.com/hc/en-us/articles/360030735453)
- [Course Admin](https://support.thinkific.com/hc/en-us/articles/360030735613)

The following roles can view **Group reports**:

- [Site Owner](https://support.thinkific.com/hc/en-us/articles/360030353794)
- [Site Admin](https://support.thinkific.com/hc/en-us/articles/360030735453)
- [Group Analyst](https://support.thinkific.com/hc/en-us/articles/360030735853)

## Progress Reports

Progress Reports can be viewed at a per-course level. You can see when each student started the course, completed the course, and last signed in, as well as their [view and completion rate](https://support.thinkific.com/hc/en-us/articles/360033331514) of the course.

#### How to View a Progress Report

1. Go to **Users**
2. Select **Student Progress**
3. Click **Progress reports** beside your desired Course name

You can choose to look at all students or search by a specific student. Just select from the User drop-down menu and click **Apply.**

You can export this data as a CSV by clicking the **Export CSV** button. CSV (comma separated values) is a universal file type that can be opened by any spreadsheet program.

## Cohort Reports

A **cohort** is a grouping of students with a common start date. For example, everyone who enrolled in your course in January would be part of the January cohort.

Cohort Reports allow you to compare student completion rates by groups of students in a **cohort**. They can be viewed at a course level and will display the number of students in each cohort, the average **%** **Complete** of a course for each cohort of students, and a timeline.

#### How to View a Cohort Report

1. Go to **Users**
2. Select **Student Progress**
3. Click the **ellipses (3 dots)** to the right of **Progress reports** beside your desired Course name

You can choose to have this exported as CSV or view it as HTML. If you are viewing the **Cohort Report (HTML)** you can change the interval from weeks to months or years - just select the period you want to look at and use the **Apply** button to activate those filters.

## Group Reports

You can also export progress per Group! Find out more about Groups and how to run Group Reports [here](https://support.thinkific.com/hc/en-us/articles/360030350854) **.**

## Important Considerations

If you add new lessons to a course that has students already enrolled, we recommend [resetting completions](https://support.thinkific.com/hc/en-us/articles/360030736393). This will cause the student's progress rate to adjust according to the existing content in your course.

For example, if you have 9 lessons in a course and a student is at 100% complete, when you add one more lesson and reset completions, the student's new progress rate will be 90%.

## Frequently Asked Questions

**Why are some students missing a date under Activated at?**

If you have a [Free Preview](https://support.thinkific.com/hc/en-us/articles/360030722473) set up for your course, students enrolled in the Free Preview will have no date under **Activated at** in the course progress report.

## About This Feature

As a Creator Educator, tracking the performance of your business can be time-consuming and complex. However, with Thinkific Analytics, you can harness the power of your data and make informed decisions to scale your business. This feature allows you to view key business metrics such as visitors and conversions, enrollment numbers, completion rates, and revenue, all presented in easy-to-use charts with clear navigation. You can also drill down and filter your data sets to get more specific answers and even export the data to share with your team or incorporate into business reports. Thinkific Analytics helps you easily understand how your learning content, business, and students are performing while enabling you to utilize this information to improve the effectiveness of your learning programs and grow your business.

- **Keep your audience engaged with actionable insights**

View your business metrics in one place, making it easy for you to gain a holistic view of your business. Easily understand how your business is performing, identify areas for improvement, and make data-driven decisions that help grow your business. Actionable insights enable you to optimize your business processes, marketing strategies, and course content to achieve better results.

- **Improve the student experience with engagement data**

Thinkific Analytics provides engagement data that enables you to identify gaps in your content and improve the student experience. By understanding what students find most engaging, you can optimize your course content to better meet their needs.

- **Prove that your work is making a difference**

Demonstrate the value and impact of your education programs. Thinkific Analytics enables you to show the health of your education programs, allowing you to connect back to retention, adoption, and engagement.

- **Understand your visitor and student behavior and optimize for success**

By analyzing your visitor, conversion, revenue, course, and engagement data, you can identify areas where your program excels and needs improvement. Analytics helps you easily launch and iterate your program based on visitor and student data findings, driving new revenue through your education program.

## Thinkific Analytics Dashboards

To access your dashboards, go to your Admin Dashboard and click on the **Analytics** section located on the left-hand side. Next, choose **Marketing**, **Enrollments, Revenue**, **Orders,** or **Engagement** from the dropdown menu.

### Marketing Dashboard

The Marketing Dashboard provides valuable insights into the performance of your marketing efforts and checkout conversions. With several dashboards that allow you to understand how each landing page is performing, how many leads you generated and where they were captured, and how many visitors turn into leads or customers, the Marketing Dashboard allows you to optimize your sales funnels and improve conversions.

Learn more about it here: [Thinkific Analytics: Marketing Dashboards](https://support.thinkific.com/hc/en-us/articles/23772050586519).

![Thnkific Analytics - Marketing Leads v1 - May 24.jpg](https://support.thinkific.com/hc/article_attachments/23774091666583)

### Enrollments Dashboard

The Enrollments dashboard provides valuable insights into the performance of your courses and communities and allows you to understand which learning products your audience values most over time. The dashboard offers a comprehensive view of your enrollments, featuring a main line graph that visualizes enrollments over time.

The data can be further filtered by time period, enrollment type, product name, and group name, allowing you to identify trends and patterns that inform your decision-making. In addition, pie charts are included to show the percentage of enrollments started and completed, providing a clear picture of engagement and retention rates.

> At the moment, the Enrollments Dashboard does not include [scheduled enrollments](https://support.thinkific.com/hc/en-us/articles/360030351374).

![](https://support.thinkific.com/hc/article_attachments/13463316720023)

The List of Enrollments table at the bottom of this page features detailed information about your enrollments and student details including if a certificate was issued and the certificate expiry date.

If you would like to generate an enrollment list including the Group each student is in, this is available when exporting a CSV report. See details here: [Can I view the Group my student is in from the Enrollments Dashboard in Thinkific Analytics?](https://support.thinkific.com/hc/en-us/articles/16486333152023)

### Orders Dashboard

The Orders Dashboard provides valuable insights into your completed orders over time, enabling you to optimize your sales strategies on Thinkific and effectively target your audience. With a main line graph illustrating order trends and a pie chart differentiating orders from new and returning students, this dashboard offers a comprehensive overview. Key metrics such as _Total Orders_, _Unique Students_, and _Top Products By Orders_ provide a snapshot of performance for the selected time period and products. Additionally, the dashboard presents a breakdown of orders based on product type, coupons used, payment type, payment provider, and customer classification as new or returning. These insights equip you with the necessary data to analyze and enhance your selling approach.

![](https://support.thinkific.com/hc/article_attachments/13344458705431)

### Revenue Dashboards

#### Revenue Overview

The Revenue Overview Dashboard provides a comprehensive overview of your business's revenue trends over time. With a main line graph and key metrics such as _Total Revenue_, _Average Revenue per User_, and _Number of Transactions_, the dashboard allows you to understand the factors driving your revenue and make informed decisions to plan, optimize, and grow your business.

All revenues reported are presented as gross revenue, meaning that the figures represent the total amount paid by the student, inclusive of taxes and processing fees. The actual revenue earned, which excludes taxes and fees, may differ.

![](https://support.thinkific.com/hc/article_attachments/13344446535703)

The Revenue Overview Dashboard displays in USD by default. If you have [changed your site currency](https://support.thinkific.com/hc/en-us/articles/360038136513), you will see a dropdown menu at the top of the dashboard that you can use to switch from USD to your local currency.![](https://support.thinkific.com/guide-media/01H6EW9NKV07Y1Y68QWP4MXNFT)

#### TCommerce Dashboards

> Adopt [Thinkific Payments](https://support.thinkific.com/hc/en-us/articles/360062084913) in order to access and leverage all the integrated business management tools available on [TCommerce](https://support.thinkific.com/hc/en-us/articles/7302902582807), including the Subscriptions and Revenue Dashboards.

The TCommerce Subscriptions and Revenue Dashboards track the number of active subscriptions and total revenue broken down by payment type and date range, to monitor customer engagement and identify trends. Evaluate the effectiveness of your acquisition efforts by analyzing the number of new and churned subscriptions and the overall revenue during specific time periods. Monitor your Monthly Recurring Revenue (MRR) growth and changes over time to help you assess the financial health of your business. Additionally, keep a close eye on revenue retention to identify areas for learner retention improvements. Use these insights to make informed decisions, optimize your pricing strategies, and implement targeted retention initiatives to foster sustainable business growth

Learn more about [TCommerce: Subscriptions and Revenue Dashboard](https://support.thinkific.com/hc/en-us/articles/15197865547543/).

### Engagement Dashboards

Keep your audience engaged with actionable insights. The Engagement Dashboards provide you with separate dashboards - Overview, Course, Community, Video, Quiz, Survey, Student Performance, SCORM, and Thinker - to help you grow your business smarter. Get the data and insights you need to measure student engagement, revenue, and overall program performance while empowering your team to make impactful decisions.

Learn more about [Thinkific Analytics: Engagement Dashboards](https://support.thinkific.com/hc/en-us/articles/15795863469463)

![](https://support.thinkific.com/hc/article_attachments/15919085668887)

## Advanced Analytics

Advanced Analytics is only available on Thinkific Plus.

##### Curious about Plus?

##### Book a call with our experts to see how it can supercharge your business growth!

[Book a call](https://www.thinkific.com/plus/plus-customer-enquiries/?utm_source=knowledge_base&utm_medium=help_center&utm_campaign=advanced_analytics)

Unlock the full potential of your data with Advanced Analytics. Tailor your insights by effortlessly building, adjusting, and customizing your data views to match your unique needs. Plus, with Advanced Analytics you can streamline your reporting process by scheduling and sending reports directly to you or other stakeholders via email, webhooks or other preferred formats.

Learn more about it here: [Thinkific Analytics: Advanced Analytics](https://support.thinkific.com/hc/en-us/articles/24661935433367)

![](https://support.thinkific.com/hc/article_attachments/24918187201559)

## Giving Users Access to Your Dashboards

Access this feature on Thinkific Plus!

##### Curious, but not yet on Plus?

##### Discover how Plus can supercharge your business growth.

[Book a call](https://www.thinkific.com/plus/plus-customer-enquiries/?utm_source=knowledge_base&utm_medium=help_center&utm_campaign=analytics_access)

As a [Site Owner](https://support.thinkific.com/hc/en-us/articles/360030353794), you have the ability to assign roles to users, granting them access to the Course, Quiz, Student Performance, Survey, and SCORM [Engagement Dashboards](https://support.thinkific.com/hc/en-us/articles/15795863469463), as well as the [Enrollments Dashboard](https://support.thinkific.com/hc/en-us/articles/360040037093-Thinkific-Analytics#enrollments) within Thinkific Analytics. By designating individuals as [Site Admins](https://support.thinkific.com/hc/en-us/articles/360030735453), [Course Admins](https://support.thinkific.com/hc/en-us/articles/360030735613), or [Group Analysts](https://support.thinkific.com/hc/en-us/articles/360030735853), you can provide them with tailored insights and empower them to make data-driven decisions.

Site Admins get comprehensive access to all Thinkific Analytics dashboards, enabling them to gain a holistic view of engagement across the entire platform. Group Analysts and Course Admins have restricted access to Engagement and Enrollment Dashboards, limiting their visibility to data specific to the Course or Group they are associated with.

This feature allows designated users to analyze engagement metrics, identify trends, and optimize their respective courses or groups to foster better student outcomes. By granting selective access to the Engagement Dashboards, you can distribute responsibilities, collaborate effectively, and enhance the overall learning experience for your students.

## AI Dashboard Summarization

> This feature is available on the Grow plan and above.

Get insights into your data faster with the AI-powered dashboard summarization. This feature allows you to use AI to summarize the currently displayed data on your dashboard and display a summary description with bullet point takeaways. The AI summary helps consolidate findings and speed up the time required to turn data into meaningful insights.

To use the AI dashboard summarization:

1. Open the dashboard you want to summarize (Enrollments or Course Engagement)
2. Filter data to the desired results
3. Click the **AI Summary** button
4. See the results
5. Adjust filters and re-run summary, as needed

#### Important Considerations

- The AI dashboard summarization feature is currently in beta.
- The AI dashboard summarization feature is available to customers on the Grow plan and above.
- The AI dashboard summarization feature is only available on the **Enrollment dashboard** and the **Engagement: Course dashboard**.
- The feature analyzes results _currently displayed in the dashboard_. This means that using filters will impact what data is analyzed.
- All currently displayed results will be used in the analysis, except for single-value tiles and per lesson / enrollment detail tiles, as these offer little narrative value.

![Analytics - AI Summary - Feb 26.jpg](https://support.thinkific.com/hc/article_attachments/38137781927959)

## Filter Your Data

Thinkific Analytic filters allow you to focus on what's important by eliminating irrelevant data to help you save time and increase productivity. Filtering is flexible and can be adjusted as new data becomes available or when needs change.

### How to Filter Your Data

1. From your Admin Dashboard, select **Analytics**
2. Choose the Dashboard you want to get data from

5. Click on the **Update** button in the top-right corner

![](https://support.thinkific.com/hc/article_attachments/13403899489943)

## Export Your Data

Exporting your data enables you to use a variety of other tools to conduct a deeper analysis and interpretation of data, revealing trends and patterns that may have gone unnoticed. Exporting your data also allows you to revisit and reanalyze them at your convenience for ongoing learning and knowledge building to give you a greater understanding of your business over time.

### How to Export a Full Dataset

> You have the option to export full datasets as a CVS or PDF file.

1. Select a dataset to export
2. _Optional:_ Filter your dashboard to isolate a subset of data
3. Click **Dashboard actions** (ellipses) in the top-right corner then, **Download**
4. Select your _Format_ using the dropdown menu and click **Download**

![](https://support.thinkific.com/hc/article_attachments/13404480012695)

### How to Export a Partial Dataset

> You have the option to export partial datasets in TXT, Excel, CVS, JSON, HTML, Markdown, and PNG formats.

1. Select a dataset to export
2. _Optional:_ Filter your dashboard to isolate a subset of data
3. Select a tile on your dashboard and click **Tile actions** (ellipses) then, **Download data**
4. Select your _Format_ using the dropdown menu
5. _Optional:_ Configure your data set further using **Advanced data options**
6. Click **Download**

**![](https://support.thinkific.com/hc/article_attachments/13404480610455)**

## Frequently Asked Questions

**Why don't I see any data on my dashboard?**

Dashboards that do not have any data will appear blank. For example, if you only have Free courses on your site, you will see a blank dashboard for Revenue. Make sure to check back when you start accepting payment!

Another instance where users may not see any data on their dashboards is when a user is a [Course Admin](https://support.thinkific.com/hc/en-us/articles/360030735613) or [Group Analyst](https://support.thinkific.com/hc/en-us/articles/360030735853). If they have not been assigned a course or a group, or if the user is both a Course Admin and Group Analyst but either role is missing an assigned course or group, the user will not see any data in the Analytics reports.

**Why isn't all of my student data displaying on the dashboard?**

If a student has recently enrolled or progressed through a course chapter or lesson, you may need to wait up to 24 hours to see this activity in your [engagement reports](https://support.thinkific.com/hc/en-us/articles/360040037093-Thinkific-Analytics#studentengagement). Reporting data is updated daily at 4 a.m. PDT.

Another possible reason student data may be missing is that a student's enrollment did not occur during the date range selected. We suggest adjusting the date range and re-checking the data.

Listed datasets, such as a list of Transactions, Orders, and Enrollments, have a maximum display limit of 5000 rows of data. To access the full data set, we recommend exporting the data. Make sure to select **All Results** under _Advanced data options_ before exporting the dataset.

**I added content to my course and my student completion and progress rates went down. What happened?**

If you add lessons to a course with existing enrollments, students working on the course will now have more lessons to complete. As a result, the percentage of your course that they have already completed will decrease.

This is automatically reflected in the Student Engagement Reporting, which will dynamically update student progress and completion percentages when you add or remove course content. The report will automatically refresh to reflect any changes.

If you add or remove lesson content from your courses, you will need to [recalculate the progress](https://support.thinkific.com/hc/en-us/articles/360030736393) in the [Progress Report](https://support.thinkific.com/hc/en-us/articles/360030369974) to see up-to-date progress and completion percentages in the report.

Please note that it may take up to 24 hours for your reports to update when you add or remove new course content. Reporting data is updated daily at 4 a.m. PDT.

**Can I report on purchases made outside of Thinkific?**

Our analytics feature only applies to purchases made through [Thinkific Payments](https://support.thinkific.com/hc/en-us/articles/360062084913), [Stripe](https://support.thinkific.com/hc/en-us/articles/360030723513), and [PayPal](https://support.thinkific.com/hc/en-us/articles/360030357494). If you are using an [external payment solution](https://support.thinkific.com/hc/en-us/articles/360035692594), the [Revenue](https://support.thinkific.com/hc/en-us/articles/360040037093-Thinkific-Analytics#revenueinsights) and [Orders Dashboards](https://support.thinkific.com/hc/en-us/articles/360040037093-Thinkific-Analytics#ordersinsights) will not display data, however, you can still refer to the [Enrollments](https://support.thinkific.com/hc/en-us/articles/360040037093-Thinkific-Analytics#enrollmentinsights) and [Student Engagement Dashboards](https://support.thinkific.com/hc/en-us/articles/360040037093-Thinkific-Analytics#studentengagement)!

**What is the difference between the Order Details Report and the Revenue Details Report?**

The Orders Report will only show the unique order at the time of purchase. It does not include each individual transaction for subsequent subscription and payment plan payments.

The Revenue Report will show the total revenue of each individual transaction to date.

**What is the difference between the Thinkific Analytics Orders Dashboard and the Orders Report under Sales?**

The [Thinkific Analytics Orders Dashboard](https://support.thinkific.com/hc/en-us/articles/360040037093-Thinkific-Analytics#ordersreport) is meant to help you analyze trends. This means it provides an overview of total orders in a time period and helps answer questions like:

- How many unique students are ordering?
- Which products a have higher order count?
- How many orders are from new versus returning customers?

The [Orders Report](https://support.thinkific.com/hc/en-us/articles/360030736233) available in **Sales** is focused on transaction-based data to help with accounting or reviewing specific order details. This report is available on [all Thinkific plans](https://www.thinkific.com/pricing/).

[\[back to top\]](https://support.thinkific.com/hc/en-us/articles/360040037093-Thinkific-Analytics#top)

[Next article Why do I have missing order numbers?](https://support.thinkific.com/hc/en-us/articles/360030358114-Why-do-I-have-missing-order-numbers)

web.title: How do I track student engagement? - Thinkific Support
web.description: There are many different ways to check in on how students are engaging with your content. Find which method for tracking engagement works best for you!
web.markdown:
[Skip to main content](https://support.thinkific.com/hc/en-us/articles/360030351934-How-do-I-track-student-engagement#main-content)

Plan Availability- image


Legacy Plans- image


Platform- image


_There are many different ways to check in on how students are engaging with your content. Find which method for tracking engagement works best for you!_

* * *

## In this article:

[Thinkific Analytics: Engagement Dashboards](https://support.thinkific.com/hc/en-us/articles/360030351934-How-do-I-track-student-engagement#analytics)

[Progress](https://support.thinkific.com/hc/en-us/articles/360030351934-How-do-I-track-student-engagement#progress)

[Activity Feed](https://support.thinkific.com/hc/en-us/articles/360030351934-How-do-I-track-student-engagement#activity)

[Sign-in Information](https://support.thinkific.com/hc/en-us/articles/360030351934-How-do-I-track-student-engagement#sign)

[Quiz & Survey Results](https://support.thinkific.com/hc/en-us/articles/360030351934-How-do-I-track-student-engagement#quiz)

[Video Analytics](https://support.thinkific.com/hc/en-us/articles/360030351934-How-do-I-track-student-engagement#video)

[Discussions](https://support.thinkific.com/hc/en-us/articles/360030351934-How-do-I-track-student-engagement#discussions)

[Additional Tracking Options](https://support.thinkific.com/hc/en-us/articles/360030351934-How-do-I-track-student-engagement#add)

## Thinkific Analytics: Engagement Dashboards

The Engagement Dashboards are a powerful component of [Thinkific Analytics](https://support.thinkific.com/hc/en-us/articles/360040037093) to help you grow your business smarter. The Engagement Dashboards provide you with multiple separate dashboards - from Video to Quiz and Survey to SCORM and more - to help you grow your business smarter. Get the data and insights you need to measure student engagement, revenue, and overall program performance while empowering your team to make impactful decisions.

Learn more: [Thinkific Analytics: Engagement Dashboards](https://support.thinkific.com/hc/en-us/articles/15795863469463)

## Progress

As students complete lessons and chapters inside courses, we track their Progress as a percentage. Let's say the student completes one out of ten lessons; the student's Progress will be marked as 10% complete. A lesson is marked as complete specifically when the student clicks the "Complete & Continue" button on a lesson. Once doing so, the student will see a checkmark in the [Course Player](https://support.thinkific.com/hc/en-us/articles/360030372514) sidebar beside the lesson name. Students can see their progress within the course, and [Site Admins](https://support.thinkific.com/hc/en-us/articles/360030735453) can run Progress reports to see this data. To see how to run these reports, check out [Progress Reports.](https://support.thinkific.com/hc/en-us/articles/360030369974)

[\[back to top\]](https://support.thinkific.com/hc/en-us/articles/360030351934-How-do-I-track-student-engagement#top)

## Activity Feed

We provide you with an Activity feed for each individual student. When you're logged in as the Site Owner or Site Admin, you can view a student's Activity feed to see what lessons they have viewed and completed with a date stamp. The Activity Feed is great to use if you are curious which lessons your student last accessed.

#### To view an Activity feed for a student:

1. From your Admin Dashboard, select **Users**
2. Select **All Users**
3. Click on the desired **Student name**
4. Select the **Activity Feed** tab

[\[back to top\]](https://support.thinkific.com/hc/en-us/articles/360030351934-How-do-I-track-student-engagement#top)

## Sign-in Information

We provide you with both the last sign-in date and sign-in count (total number of times a student has signed in) for each student within a Users Report. To run a Users report, check out [Users Report and Filters](https://support.thinkific.com/hc/en-us/articles/360030370254).

[\[back to top\]](https://support.thinkific.com/hc/en-us/articles/360030351934-How-do-I-track-student-engagement#top)

## Quiz & Survey Results

If you have quizzes or surveys inside your courses you can find out which students have completed them and view their answers. [See here for how to export your Quiz & Survey data.](https://support.thinkific.com/hc/en-us/articles/360030370334) Many course creators also use surveys to ask students to provide feedback on the course. This is a great way to continually listen to your customers and improve on your learning offering!

You can see the results of Quizzes and Surveys directly in Thinkific using [Thinkific Analytics: Engagement Dashboards](https://support.thinkific.com/hc/en-us/articles/15795863469463).

[\[back to top\]](https://support.thinkific.com/hc/en-us/articles/360030351934-How-do-I-track-student-engagement#top)

## Video Analytics

For videos hosted in Thinkific, we provide video metrics at a per video level that will provide an overview of how students are interacting with your video content. You will see the following video analytics:

- **Average Engagement** \- the percentage of the video that is watched on average
- **Total Plays**\- the total number of times a video has been played
- **Play Rate**\- percentage calculated by total people who played the video divided by the total number of people who loaded this video on a webpage
- **Last Watched** \- the last date the video was watched

Learn more here: [View Video Analytics](https://support.thinkific.com/hc/en-us/articles/360030375574)

[\[back to top\]](https://support.thinkific.com/hc/en-us/articles/360030351934-How-do-I-track-student-engagement#top)

## Discussions

Discussions are a great way to see how students are interacting with each other and your content. Discussions within your course can be moderated. Find out more here: [On-Lesson Discussions](https://support.thinkific.com/hc/en-us/articles/360030351434)

[\[back to top\]](https://support.thinkific.com/hc/en-us/articles/360030351934-How-do-I-track-student-engagement#top)

## Additional Tracking Options

If you are looking for more detailed analytics for student activity, we recommend [integrating with Mixpanel](https://support.thinkific.com/hc/en-us/articles/360030731793) as this will give you a detailed breakdown of lesson activity per student. Here is an example of the breakdown you can see in Mixpanel:

![](https://support.thinkific.com/hc/article_attachments/360042078674)

Learn how to set this up here: [Integrate with Mixpanel](https://support.thinkific.com/hc/en-us/articles/360030731793)

[\[back to top\]](https://support.thinkific.com/hc/en-us/articles/360030351934-How-do-I-track-student-engagement#top)

web.description: Are you ready to Learn Thinkific? In this video, Lucas Marino, Thinkific Expert, dives into tracking learner progress in Thinkific.
web.markdown:
![Thumbnail (1920x1080)](https://i.ytimg.com/vi/5rlBGFdNixk/maxresdefault.jpg)
# [View Learner Progress in Thinkific](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=5rlBGFdNixk)

**Visibility**: Public
**Uploaded by**: [Lucas Marino | Marino Training](http://www.youtube.com/@marinotraining)
**Uploaded at**: 2022-12-26T08:38:25-08:00
**Published at**: 2022-12-26T08:38:25-08:00
**Length**: 09:27
**Views**: 605
**Likes**: 6
**Category**: People & Blogs

## Description

```
Are you ready to Learn Thinkific? In this video, Lucas Marino, Thinkific Expert, dives into tracking learner progress in Thinkific. Keeping up with the progress of your students is critical for online course providers. Learn how to track performance in Thinkific in this informative demo video.

Step-by-step instructions on how to track student performance and all features and functions.

Have you developed course material but are intimidated or slowed by learning Thinkific? Want to avoid paying someone to do the Thinkific work by learning the system yourself? Perhaps you want to build courses for others. You want to learn everything about this amazing platform to create a new line of business.

This is exactly why Lucas built Learn Thinkific - A Complete Guide to the Thinkific Learning Management System. 

This course covers every function and feature of Thinkific so you can build amazing courses, site pages, and communities. The course includes over 60 demo videos and LIVE Q&A sessions for all enrolled students.

Learn more here: https://www.marinotraining.com/courses/learn-thinkific

Join our weekly newsletter here: https://keap.app/contact-us/1762730614586645
```

## Transcript

hello there how are you welcome to the lesson on progress so if you want to see what your students are doing for progress in your courses or progress as a group you can come here to your support students tab and you scroll down to progress and when it opens when this when the student progress screen opens you're going to have a choice between viewing progress by course or viewing progress by group and we're going to go over the course functions first so let's say I opened up my progress and I come here to courses and I want to know how everybody in the maintenance task analysis course here is doing I have a few options I can come straight to that course and go down to the progress reports tab here and press this button or I can click on these ellipses now I'm going to show you the ellipses first if I click on the ellipses it gives me the opportunity to run one of two types of reports and that's a cohort reporting HTML or a cohort report in CSV I don't get confused with the whole cohort part of this it's everyone that's enrolled in this class is going to be on these reports so if you have 30 people enrolled in this course when you click uh cohort report HTML or cohort report CSV all of those students uh and their progress will be listed on those files now what if I don't want to necessarily run a report and have a file link emailed to me and have a you know a file external to thinkific what if I just want to view them quickly on screen well there's no reason to run the reports in the ellipses just come here to progress reports button and click it and it'll open up and show you all of the information that'll end up on the CSV or HTML report it shows what the viewing percentage for each student is how much of the course have they viewed how much of the course have they completed their name their email whether they've completed the course or not if they have there'll be a date here if they have not it would be blank like you see here uh when they started the course when they were activated in the system and the last time they signed in now this is really important if you're looking at like student engagement student accountability those types of things you may say well I want to make sure that my students are logging in regularly this dude hasn't logged into since October 14th and here we are in the middle of December I've got to reach out to this guy and find out why he's not necessarily where I expect him to be at this point in the course now depending on how you've designed your course um depends on whether this is really an issue we'll talk about that toward the end of this lesson I just wanted to show you it's capable here of seeing that and then of course if I have a company name loaded for them this system I'll be able to see that as well now uh just like in the ellipses on the previous page you can run a file here external to thinkific you can run an export CSV file just click that button and you'll get your little friendly thinkific notification that they've queued your report and you'll get a email when it's ready for you okay now that's how basic course student progress by course is viewed and you can see all the courses that are published in the system here now what if I want to see the progress for a group of students if I click on groups it's going to take me to all of the designated groups that I've created in thinkific under the groups tab here under support your students and this is why I mentioned in the groups lesson that it's it's nice to have students organized in groups sometimes because I might just want to zero in on a certain class of students um you know certain categories students uh people enrolled from a specific company whatever the case may be this is really big with like my B2B clients I'll enroll their students in a course with tons of other people but I want to pull a report specific to those students I can just create a group for them and pull their progress report individually now if I don't have any progress to report like if students are enrolled but they haven't done anything no one's done anything to report you're going to get this notification here there's nothing to report but if I go to my groups and I click on one that actually has students and they're doing things and I click on the button there I'm going to get a um yeah this is the Lucas Moreno fan club I'm going to get a progress report for every course that person is enrolled in so you can see here that this version of Lucas Marino is enrolled in the course tutorial and then there's nothing else for for them listed right here but as we get down to the maintenance task analysis course they're also enlisted there I can see their progress for both of those courses because I've got them in a group that person is in a group so this is the difference or one of the benefits of viewing people in groups rather than in courses in courses I only see their progress in that one course but if I have a student enrolled in multiple courses and I click on a group that they're in I'll see all of their course progress not just their one course progress so this is pretty cool it's a it's a way for you to see um things by group and again I'm a big fan of doing this for like my business to business clients if I have a company call me and want to enroll their people like Merino Consulting Services here put this dude in this class um I can say to their manager hey uh this week's uh progress report looks pretty good you might want to give this guy a nudge he hasn't logged in and done much his last sign in was oh I don't know never um meaning he hasn't really logged into the course to do anything his account was activated on December 16th but he hasn't logged into any of his courses and done any of his work okay and maybe he logged in in October right let's say the same person here was working for Mourinho Consulting Services I could say dude your guy hasn't logged in since October 14th and here we are in mid-December so it's really cool for you to be able to run these reports if I'm going to send a report to a manager at a company or whatever I'm gonna pull the X the CSV file clean up the CSV file and then forward that to them um that's kind of how I share that with managers uh training managers training directors and companies that fund training through my company okay so those are the basics of student progress by course type or by group and if you have any more questions you can always drop a comment in the discussion at the bottom of your or at uh in your course player there if you don't want to put it in the discussion you want to just read about it you can click on the ellipses in your student progress window and you can click on about this feature and it'll take you to a help article you can also come down here as always to The Help Center and search progress reports in The Help Center okay guys uh last thing I'm going to share with you before we leave is um some advice about these reports you want to be very careful about who you share them with because these reports might have multiple people on them and their progress and some people are going to be sensitive to that so if you do forward a CSV file to someone with the progress report make sure you tell them uh in the file that it might be business sensitive or it might be um it might it might be sensitive it also has people's email addresses on it and in some instances some countries some businesses some Industries uh that could be sensitive information so you also want to be careful with that so I gave you a recommendation earlier to share the CSV file with the manager um just know that when you're sharing this data with them um that you're sharing all of that information with them and it's usually not a big problem for business to business work uh it might be a problem if you're sending it to a to a contractor or consultant that you've hired to help you with your business and not necessarily related to the company who hired you to train their clients right so you just got to be careful that you're not sharing information that they've contractually asked you not to share so if you're like if I was going to send this to a uh a virtual assistant or to a partner of mine I might pull the CSV file and I might leave the first and last name but scrub the email addresses for those students right just because they don't need it it's floating these people's pii out there um and maybe their company isn't cool with that so just uh just a little note there if you do B2B work um something to be aware of when you share progress reports okay guys that is it for this lesson and I look forward to seeing you in the next
